sections.paragraph: plur. זְמַנֶּים m. time, especially a stated time (Arabic زَمَنُ, زَمَانُ time. Syr. ܙܒܰܢ id.), Ecc 3:1, לַכֹּל זְמָן “its own time for every thing,” i.e. every thing remains only so long, all things are frail and fleeting, Neh 2:6 Est 9:27, 31 . It is a word of a later age used instead of the more ancient עֵת. [This remark (omitted in Thes.) takes for granted what cannot be admitted, that Solomon did not write the book of Ecclesiastes.]
